codeReview.summary: The fix correctly flips DefaultPolicyDropdown dispatches/tests from request.* to respond.*, but it does not fully close the original failed review because dropdown-owned cells can still clobber later user-authored respond overrides on subsequent default switches.

issues.description: Original failure #2 remains: `hasUserOverride` treats a non-null respond override as non-user-owned whenever its key is still present in `defaultAppliedKeys`, but there is no ownership-transfer path that removes keys when the user later edits that same cell via another respond-writing surface. On the next default change, `applyDefault` unsets all `priorKeys` first, so those user-authored values can still be reverted by dropdown cleanup instead of being preserved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still documents peer chips as `respond.*`, but implementation and this fix's audit comment treat peer chips as `request.*` while the default dropdown targets `respond.*`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md lines 107-109 say PeerPolicyChip targets respond.*, but src/screens/DashboardScreen/panels/PeerPolicyChip.tsx (added audit comment in commit ae8509a) states chips write request.* and defaults to `direction?: ...` with request default.

summary: FAIL: Reviewed fix commit ae8509a, its handoff/transcript skeleton, prior review, and both feature diffs. Direction semantics are corrected to respond.*, but the prior override-clobbering failure mode is not fully resolved.
